content:
{
  "title": "Dynamic Unit-Checking Prompting for Robust Mathematical Reasoning in LLMs",
  "problem": "LLMs frequently make errors in mathematical problem solving due to failures in tracking and manipulating physical units, leading to logically plausible but physically invalid answers. Existing prompting methods do not systematically ensure consistency of units throughout multi-step reasoning.",
  "motivation": "Unit consistency is a fundamental aspect of mathematical and scientific problem solving, and humans rely on units as a robust check against reasoning mistakes. As LLMs are increasingly used for STEM education and engineering support, addressing errors caused by unit mismanagement can significantly improve the reliability of automated mathematical reasoning.",
  "method": "We propose a novel prompting technique called Dynamic Unit-Checking Prompting (DUCP), which interleaves unit annotations and explicit unit-consistency verification steps into the model's chain-of-thought reasoning. At each computation step, the prompt requests the model to annotate the units of intermediate and final results, then to verify unit compatibility before proceeding to the next operation. This is enforced through structured templates and in-context exemplars illustrating correct and incorrect unit handling. DUCP is model-agnostic and can be applied to any LLM via prompt engineering, without additional training.",
  "experiment_plan": "We evaluate DUCP on GSM8K, MATH, and competition-level physics problems, comparing against standard CoT and MathPrompter variants. Metrics include answer accuracy, intermediate step correctness, and explicit unit-consistency error rates. Human and automated unit-checkers are used to assess unit handling in generated outputs."
}
